Risk Description: Because of recurring environment downtimes totaling 181 hours of team productivity loss since May 14, 2026 (documented in both WSR and JIRA-1009), and network issues causing Enterprise services disruptions in DEV/QA environments, there is a risk that development, testing, and integration activities will continue to be severely disrupted, which could lead to cascading schedule delays, reduced code quality, missed milestones, and increased project costs due to extended timelines and resource inefficiency.
Potential Impact: Continued productivity loss impacting 181+ hours; cascading schedule delays across all releases; reduced code quality due to interrupted development cycles; team morale degradation; increased project costs from extended timelines; potential SLA breaches; delayed Release 1 go-live.

SLA Metric: Defect Leakage: ~4.8%; Defect Density: 22.19 bugs/KLOC vs target 2 bugs/KLOC; 116 QA defects
Risk Title: Critical Quality and Defect Leakage Risk
Risk Description: Because of 116 QA defects identified across sprints 15-20 with a defect leakage rate of approximately 4.8% (approaching the critical 5% threshold documented in WSR), and actual defect density of 22.19 bugs per KLOC exceeding the target of 2 bugs per KLOC by over 10x (documented in JIRA-1024), there is a risk that systemic code quality issues will result in escaped defects reaching UAT and production, which could lead to customer-reported issues, production incidents, extensive rework, SLA breaches, increased support costs, and reputational damage.
Potential Impact: Customer-reported production defects; UAT delays and quality concerns; production incidents impacting end users; extensive rework consuming development capacity; SLA breaches; increased support and maintenance costs; reputational damage; potential contract penalties.

SLA Metric: UAT paused; 51 open bugs (12 High, 39 Medium); Readiness assessment 0.1-0.4 required
Risk Title: UAT Readiness and Regression Coverage Risk
Risk Description: Because UAT for Release 0.4+0.8 was paused (documented in WSR) and a complete UAT readiness assessment from Release 0.1 through 0.4 is required before re-release to Axos (documented in JIRA-1008), and there are currently 51 open bugs (12 High priority, 39 Medium priority) with quality gates needing strengthening, there is a risk that accumulated technical debt, incomplete regression testing coverage (currently 80% automated, 20% manual), and unresolved defects will surface during UAT, which could lead to extended UAT cycles, delayed go-live, scope reduction, and customer dissatisfaction.
Potential Impact: Extended UAT cycles delaying Release 1 go-live; increased defect discovery during UAT requiring extensive rework; potential scope reduction to meet timelines; customer dissatisfaction with quality; compressed testing windows for subsequent releases; increased QA costs.

Risk Description: Because performance requirements are stringent (UI page rendering ≤10 seconds, API response time ≤3 seconds documented in WSR) and performance testing strategy discussions are ongoing (documented in JIRA-1017), with API and workload analysis still in progress, there is a risk that performance testing will be inadequate, delayed, or reveal significant performance gaps requiring code refactoring or hardware capacity enhancements, which could lead to failure to meet performance SLAs, delayed go-live, customer dissatisfaction, and potential need for costly infrastructure upgrades.
Potential Impact: Performance SLA breaches impacting user experience; code refactoring required causing schedule delays; hardware capacity upgrades increasing costs; delayed go-live pending performance optimization; customer dissatisfaction with system responsiveness; potential contract penalties for SLA violations.

SLA Metric: 5 beta customers data migration required; POC on Chart of Accounts in progress
Risk Title: Data Migration Strategy and Execution Risk
Risk Description: Because data migration strategy and estimates are not yet finalized (documented in WSR as 'progress slow; ETA-TBD'), and the ability to have 5 beta customers ready at Release 1 go-live depends on successful data migration from Legacy Zenith to NexLedgr (documented in JIRA-1018), with POC on Chart of Accounts still in progress and resource onboarding/training ongoing, there is a risk that data migration delays, quality issues, or incomplete migration planning will prevent timely customer onboarding, which could lead to delayed go-live, data quality issues requiring extensive reconciliation, potential manual data entry as fallback, and increased project costs.
Tech Stack: Data Migration Tools, SQL Server 2019, ETL
Potential Impact: Delayed customer onboarding preventing Release 1 launch; go-live delays impacting business timelines; data quality issues requiring extensive reconciliation and rework; potential manual data entry increasing costs and error risk; customer dissatisfaction; increased project costs from extended timelines.

SLA Metric: 4 weeks delay post AWS/FMS setup; AWS Bedrock setup pending
Risk Title: AI Infrastructure Dependency and Delay Risk
Risk Description: Because of pending AWS Bedrock setup, ADO to AWS pipeline integration, and FMS configuration for Billie Part 2 AI invoice processing using Claude Sonnet 4.5 (documented in JIRA-1002), and AXOS infrastructure team has enabled Bedrock services but service accounts testing is pending (documented in WSR as 'can be tested on 18th March once pipeline integration completed'), there is a risk that development and QA completion will be delayed by at least 4 weeks after infrastructure is ready, which could lead to UAT start date postponement, AI-driven invoice processing unavailable for beta customers, and impact to Release 1 delivery timeline.
Tech Stack: AWS Bedrock, Claude Sonnet 4.5, AI/ML, Cloud Infrastructure
Potential Impact: 4+ weeks delay in Billie Part 2 development and QA; UAT start postponement; AI-driven invoice processing unavailable for Release 1; beta customer functionality gaps; Release 1 go-live at risk; potential cost overruns from infrastructure delays; competitive disadvantage without AI capabilities.

SLA Metric: Data gateway and workspace setup pending; Requirements for SAVE/SAVE AS due 4/16
Risk Description: Because of incomplete Power BI infrastructure setup (data gateway, workspace configuration documented in JIRA-1003), and new requirements for SAVE, SAVE AS, Delete functionalities at Customer/Client/User level were finalized on 3/13 with estimated completion 4/16 (documented in WSR), and reporting POC review items may result in new design approaches requiring rework of completed deliverables, there is a risk that development end dates cannot be finalized and reporting capabilities will not be ready for UAT, which could lead to incomplete business intelligence functionality in Release 1, delayed decision-making capabilities, and potential compliance and audit risks.
Tech Stack: Power BI, Data Gateway, Angular 18, APIs
Potential Impact: Reporting functionality unavailable or incomplete for Release 1; business intelligence gaps preventing data-driven decisions; delayed decision-making capabilities for stakeholders; potential rework of completed deliverables; compliance and audit risks from missing reporting; UAT delays.

SLA Metric: User Story/UX finalization delayed; Multiple HLD approvals pending
Risk Title: Pre-Development Approval and Requirements Delay Risk
Risk Description: Because of delays in completing Pre-Development deliverables including User Stories, UX Design, Functional Requirements, and HLD approvals across multiple releases (0.2-0.8 documented in JIRA-1001), with specific delays for Release 0.4 Payments/Disbursements module (pending approvals for Functional Requirements 7/31, Design 8/8, Data Models 8/1, HLD documented in JIRA-1004), and Vendors module with revised development start of August 4 due to incomplete user research (documented in JIRA-1005), there is a risk that development start dates will be pushed back, which could lead to cascading delays in UAT timelines, compressed development and testing cycles, and overall Release 1 delivery delay beyond mid-July/August 2026 target.
Potential Impact: Cascading delays to UAT and Release 1 go-live; compressed development and testing cycles increasing defect risk; potential SLA breach on delivery timeline; increased project costs from extended timelines; resource reallocation challenges; scope compression or quality compromises.

SLA Metric: HLD approvals delayed until 7/29-7/31; Development already started
Risk Description: Because of pending HLD approvals for Approval Engine (Kannan, ETA 8/1) and Approval UI HLD (Keith, ETA 7/30), with development already started using HLD 7.2 (documented in JIRA-1006), and HLD completion and approval for Release 0.2 modules was delayed until July 29-31 while development was already complete (documented in JIRA-1010), there is a risk of significant architectural rework and misalignment between design and implementation, which could lead to defects, integration issues, security and compliance gaps in approval workflows, and schedule impacts for subsequent releases requiring extensive refactoring.
Potential Impact: Architectural rework consuming significant development capacity; integration challenges across modules; increased defect density from design-implementation gaps; potential security and compliance gaps in approval workflows; schedule impacts for future releases; increased technical debt.

SLA Metric: Comprehensive API list due 3/9; BurpSuite report extracted
Risk Description: Because public and private API categorization is in progress requiring comprehensive documentation of background processes and API endpoints (ETA 3/9 documented in JIRA-1021), and all private APIs must enforce service account token authentication with authorization scenarios requiring QA coordination, and BurpSuite security scan results have been extracted but not yet acted upon (documented in WSR and JIRA-1022), there is a risk that incomplete or incorrect API categorization will create security vulnerabilities and authorization gaps, which could lead to compliance violations, security breaches, audit failures, delayed production deployment, and reputational damage.
Potential Impact: Security vulnerabilities exposing sensitive financial data; authorization gaps allowing unauthorized access; compliance violations preventing production deployment; potential security breaches and data leaks; audit failures; delayed production deployment pending security remediation; reputational damage; potential regulatory penalties.

SLA Metric: USPS address validation decision pending; Master data upload approach under discussion
Risk Title: Third-Party Integration and Decision Delay Risk
Risk Description: Because auto-population of USPS address requires integration with third-party services and AXOS is currently evaluating existing solutions with decision still pending (documented in WSR as 'Executive Attention/Asks' and JIRA dependency), and master data load of financial institutions and account numbers requires backend structural changes that may impact already completed modules like Bank Accounts (documented in WSR dependencies), there is a risk that delayed decisions on third-party service selection and data structure approach will impact Milestone 1 delivery timelines, which could lead to workarounds, functionality gaps, rework of completed modules, and delayed customer onboarding.
Potential Impact: Milestone 1 delivery delays; functionality gaps in address validation; rework of completed Bank Accounts module; workarounds reducing user experience quality; delayed customer onboarding; potential scope reduction; increased integration costs.

Financial Metric: Fixed price: Schedule B; Hourly rate: $60-120/hr; 300 Accrued Hours available (25 hrs/month)
Risk Title: Budget Overrun and Change Management Risk
Risk Description: Because Phase 2 operates under a fixed-price model with change management requiring either fixed-price quotes, hourly rates ($60-120/hr), or use of 300 Accrued Hours (25 hrs/month) (documented in JIRA-1012), and multiple scope changes have occurred including new SAVE/SAVE AS requirements (3/13), UI revamp using Play+ framework (Phase 1: 7/23-8/7, Phase 2: 8/11-9/8), reporting POC review items potentially requiring rework, and data model changes causing rework in Milestone 1 (documented in WSR and JIRA-1023), there is a risk that accumulated scope changes, requirement additions, and rework will exceed available Accrued Hours and require additional budget approvals, which could lead to cost overruns, contract disputes, scope compression, potential descoping of features, and stakeholder dissatisfaction.
Potential Impact: Budget overruns exceeding fixed-price and Accrued Hours allocation; contract disputes over change management costs; scope compression to fit budget constraints; potential descoping of planned features; stakeholder dissatisfaction; margin erosion; need for additional budget approvals causing delays.

SLA Metric: Multiple features deferred from R1 to R2
Risk Title: Release 1 Scope Deferral and MVP Risk
Risk Description: Because multiple features have been deferred from Release 1 to Release 2 including International Wires, ACH Confirmations, Credit Card Auto-Allocation, Dashboard Widgets, Task Management, Tickler System, Deposit Scheduling, Audit Log UI, and File Management UI (documented in JIRA-1015), and quality gates need strengthening with ongoing defect management challenges (documented in WSR), there is a risk that Release 1 will not meet minimum viable product requirements for beta customers, which could lead to delayed customer onboarding, reduced business value realization, customer dissatisfaction, and competitive disadvantage in the market.
Potential Impact: Reduced Release 1 business value and functionality; delayed customer onboarding due to missing critical features; potential customer dissatisfaction with limited capabilities; competitive disadvantage; need for interim workarounds; pressure to accelerate Release 2 delivery.

SLA Metric: Cross-POD dependencies not fully identified upfront
Risk Description: Because cross-POD dependencies were not fully identified upfront, leading to coordination challenges across Alpha, Cypher, Nexus, and Apex teams (documented in JIRA-1025), and shared services component dependencies exist with issues being unearthed (documented in WSR as 'Shared services team – issues being unearthed'), and data model changes for Deposits/Receipts/Commissions caused rework due to shared services dependencies (documented in JIRA-1023), there is a risk that integration issues, conflicting changes, and dependency delays will continue to impact delivery across multiple PODs, which could lead to schedule delays, integration defects, quality issues, and team coordination challenges requiring increased management overhead.
Potential Impact: Integration issues across PODs causing defects; conflicting changes requiring rework; dependency delays impacting multiple teams; schedule impacts cascading across releases; quality issues from integration gaps; increased management overhead for coordination; team morale challenges.

SLA Metric: Historical loss of key resources: 2x DB Architects, Solution Architect, Product Owner, Delivery Leader, Testing Lead
Risk Title: Key Resource Attrition and Knowledge Loss Risk
Risk Description: Because historical project data shows loss of key team members over the last 12 months including 2x DB Architects (Yuri, Gokhan), Solution Architect (Rakesh), Product Owner (Tina), Delivery Leader (Sudheep), and Testing Lead (Kinjal) (documented in JIRA-1026 and historical risk patterns from knowledge base), and the project has experienced skill gaps and resource challenges in the past, there is a risk that key personnel attrition will occur during critical Phase 2 delivery periods, which could lead to knowledge loss, productivity decline, schedule delays, increased onboarding costs, quality impacts, and challenges in maintaining architectural consistency and technical direction.
Potential Impact: Knowledge loss from key personnel departure; productivity decline during transition periods; schedule delays from onboarding and ramp-up; increased costs for replacement resources and knowledge transfer; quality impacts from loss of institutional knowledge; architectural inconsistency; technical direction challenges.
